title: Amiga 1000: Ten years ahead of its time
excerpt: This computer was so good and so far out there, we didn't know what we had.
content_html:
On July 23, 1985, Commodore introduced its Amiga 1000 computer. And let’s just say the world wasn’t ready for it yet. Dave Haynie, a Commodore engineer who worked on the later models, has said there was no such thing as a 1980s computer. There were 1970s computers and 1990s computers, and it was the Amiga that dragged the rest of the industry into the 1990s.

It is not hyperbole to say the Amiga 1000 was 10 years ahead of its time. Its custom chipset had color graphics with up to 4,096 colors, stereo sound, a graphical user interface. Its operating system had full preemptive multitasking. The combination of the chipset and the operating system meant it could run on a Motorola 68000 CPU clocked at 7.14 megahertz and 256 KB of RAM. And it could boot and run off a single floppy drive. Amiga demonstrated it in January 1984, when the chips existed only as prototype circuit boards. By the end of 1985, it was a shipping product.

Realistically, an ideal configuration had dual floppy drives and 512k of RAM, and a megabyte was a lot nicer.

But dual floppies and a meg of RAM wasn’t an outlandish machine in 1985, and in that configuration, it was smooth and pleasant to use. Commodore commissioned artist Andy Warhol to demonstrate it by using it to create digital art to show off its capabilities.

Multitasking: The Amiga 1000’s secret

Graphical user interfaces weren’t exactly new in the summer of 1985. Apple had introduced the Macintosh about a year and a half earlier, and the Lisa predated even that by a nearly a year. Atari released its ST computer three months before the Amiga, and the ST also had a graphical operating system with a mouse and color.

It was multitasking that put the Amiga on the map. And it wasn’t the cooperative multitasking that Windows 3.0 and 3.1 did or Apple System 7 did. It was full preemptive multitasking like Windows 95. The only thing Windows NT did that the Amiga didn’t was memory protection.

The Amiga’s operating system wasn’t based on Unix and it certainly wasn’t based on Windows NT. Its direct ancestor was an operating system called TRIPOS that originated at Cambridge University.

Adding memory protection would have been a nice touch, but the additional required hardware would have increased costs. And the Amiga at its debut was not exactly inexpensive. The base system cost $1,295 without a monitor. A color monitor, which you definitely wanted, cost another $300. Add the additional memory and a second floppy drive for another $150 to $200 each, and you soon learned the Amiga you really wanted was more like a $2,000 computer. Living in the future was expensive, but compared to the $2,495 Apple wanted for a less capable machine, the price wasn’t unreasonable.

Living with an Amiga

I’m not sure I even saw an Amiga in person until 1987, but I knew just from reading about it that I wanted one. I wasn’t able to make it happen until 1991, so I was pretty late to the game. But even in 1991, an Amiga felt like living in the future. I could load several programs and switch between them effortlessly, with the only limit being the amount of memory I had. I could connect to a BBS with a terminal program, start a download, then switch it to the background, fire up a word processor, and do my homework while the download was happening. In some cases, I could even fire up a game and play a game while a download happened in the background. I could download stuff while I played Civilization, which was pretty great.

Initially, the Amiga had a reputation for being unstable. But that was largely because early Amiga software wasn’t very well written. Amiga software was prone to ask for resources, and assume the resources were available. Instead of failing gracefully if the resources it asked for weren’t available, it would simply allocate them, and cause a guru meditation, the Amiga equivalent of a blue screen of death or kernel panic. Except it was red, not blue.

But it wasn’t long before the software developers learned how to write code that behaved in this brave new world. Certainly by the time I was in the scene in 1991, the software generally behaved very well. When I bought a Windows PC in 1994, it felt like a serious downgrade. If I treated it like an Amiga and launched a terminal program, connected to a BBS, started download, and then switched over to Microsoft Word, it didn’t always work reliably. More often than not it was fine. But something would crash often enough to make me hesitate doing something I took for granted on an Amiga.

People ask me all the time when PCs caught up. It didn’t happen all at once. And it wasn’t until 1995 that all the pieces were there.

Why the Amiga makes me mad

And that’s why I almost everything about the Amiga makes me mad. With a good product marketing team behind it, it would have been the greatest computer of all time. When anyone asks its engineers why they built it, they tell you things like they wanted to build the very best computer that they could with the technology that was available to them at the time. That’s how engineers think, so it’s a perfectly good answer, engineer to engineer. But that answer doesn’t sell. You have to come up with something about wanting to change the world. And you have to word it in a way that sounds believable if you are going to attract a mass audience. Apple does that very well. Microsoft was never as good at it, but they were better at it than Commodore.

Commodore didn’t have a Steve Jobs or even a Steve Ballmer. They had an oligarch named Irving Gould who was only interested in looting as much from the company as possible as he ran it into the ground. It is a testament to the quality of the product Commodore had that it took him a decade to do it after Jack Tramiel left the company and left him unchecked to rob it blind.

Commodore ran a few ads, but it was clear from their advertising they had no idea what they were selling. The most effective marketers were the people who bought one. Sit down for an hour with an Amiga and someone who knew how to use it, and you’d want one too. But there weren’t enough of us to sustain the platform or the company.

How the Amiga 1000 and its successors were like a kick in the face

Commodore went bankrupt less than 9 years after introducing the first Amiga. And the company they sold it to went out of business about 15 months after buying it. So instead of getting the greatest computer of all time, it felt like we were getting kicked in the face repeatedly. And then we tried to switch to Apple or Microsoft, and waiting years for them to catch up felt like getting kicked in the face by two people.

If you missed out on it, it sounds like I’m being overdramatic. I get it. Nobody knows exactly how many people got to experience it. In the end Commodore sold around 4.91 million Amigas, but at least in the United States, Amiga owners were prone to buy more than one. That makes it hard to say how many distinct owners the machine had. But the people who owned one and lived in the future waiting for the rest of the world to catch up know exactly what I’m talking about.

David Farquhar is a computer security professional, entrepreneur, and author. He has written professionally about computers since 1991, so he was writing about retro computers when they were still new. He has been working in IT professionally since 1994 and has specialized in vulnerability management since 2013. He holds Security+ and CISSP certifications. Today he blogs five times a week, mostly about retro computers and retro gaming covering the time period from 1975 to 2000.

sentences.en: On July 23, 1985, Commodore introduced its Amiga 1000 computer.
sentences.zh: 1985 年 7 月 23 日，Commodore 推出了 Amiga 1000 计算机。

sentences.en: And let’s just say the world wasn’t ready for it yet.
sentences.zh: 我们只能说世界还没有准备好。

sentences.en: Dave Haynie, a Commodore engineer who worked on the later models, has said there was no such thing as a 1980s computer.
sentences.zh: 参与后期型号开发的 Commodore 工程师戴夫·海尼 (Dave Haynie) 表示，并没有 20 世纪 80 年代的计算机这样的东西。

sentences.en: There were 1970s computers and 1990s computers, and it was the Amiga that dragged the rest of the industry into the 1990s.
sentences.zh: 有 20 世纪 70 年代的计算机和 1990 年代的计算机，正是 Amiga 将整个行业的其他产品拖入了 1990 年代。

sentences.en: A typical Amiga 1000 setup in 1985 cost nearly $2,000 at the time.
sentences.zh: 1985 年，一台典型的 Amiga 1000 设置当时的成本接近 2,000 美元。

sentences.en: Living in the future was expensive.
sentences.zh: 生活在未来是昂贵的。

sentences.en: It is not hyperbole to say the Amiga 1000 was 10 years ahead of its time.
sentences.zh: 毫不夸张地说，Amiga 1000 领先于时代 10 年。

sentences.en: Its custom chipset had color graphics with up to 4,096 colors, stereo sound, a graphical user interface.
sentences.zh: 其定制芯片组具有多达 4,096 种颜色的彩色图形、立体声和图形用户界面。

sentences.en: Its operating system had full preemptive multitasking.
sentences.zh: 它的操作系统具有完全抢占式多任务处理能力。

sentences.en: The combination of the chipset and the operating system meant it could run on a Motorola 68000 CPU clocked at 7.14 megahertz and 256 KB of RAM.
sentences.zh: 芯片组和操作系统的组合意味着它可以在主频为 7.14 兆赫的 Motorola 68000 CPU 和 256 KB RAM 上运行。

sentences.en: And it could boot and run off a single floppy drive.
sentences.zh: 它可以通过单个软盘驱动器启动和运行。

sentences.en: Amiga demonstrated it in January 1984, when the chips existed only as prototype circuit boards.
sentences.zh: Amiga 于 1984 年 1 月展示了它，当时芯片仅作为原型电路板存在。

sentences.en: By the end of 1985, it was a shipping product.
sentences.zh: 到1985年底，它已成为出货产品。

sentences.en: Realistically, an ideal configuration had dual floppy drives and 512k of RAM, and a megabyte was a lot nicer.
sentences.zh: 实际上，理想的配置是双软盘驱动器和 512k RAM，如果是 1 MB 就更好了。

blocks.html_en: But dual floppies and a meg of RAM wasn’t an outlandish machine in 1985, and in that configuration, it was smooth and pleasant to use. Commodore commissioned artist Andy Warhol to demonstrate it by using it to create digital art to show off its capabilities.

sentences.en: But dual floppies and a meg of RAM wasn’t an outlandish machine in 1985, and in that configuration, it was smooth and pleasant to use.

sentences.en: Commodore commissioned artist Andy Warhol to demonstrate it by using it to create digital art to show off its capabilities.
sentences.zh: Commodore 委托艺术家安迪·沃霍尔 (Andy Warhol) 进行演示，用它创作数字艺术来展示其功能。

sentences.zh: 但不久之后，软件开发人员就学会了如何编写在这个美丽新世界中运行的代码。

sentences.en: Certainly by the time I was in the scene in 1991, the software generally behaved very well.
sentences.zh: 当然，当我 1991 年进入现场时，该软件总体上表现得非常好。

sentences.en: When I bought a Windows PC in 1994, it felt like a serious downgrade.
sentences.zh: 当我在 1994 年购买一台 Windows PC 时，感觉就像是一次严重的降级。

sentences.en: If I treated it like an Amiga and launched a terminal program, connected to a BBS, started download, and then switched over to Microsoft Word, it didn’t always work reliably.
sentences.zh: 如果我把它当作 Amiga 并启动终端程序，连接到 BBS，开始下载，然后切换到 Microsoft Word，它并不总是可靠地工作。

sentences.en: More often than not it was fine.
sentences.zh: 大多数情况下都很好。

sentences.en: But something would crash often enough to make me hesitate doing something I took for granted on an Amiga.
sentences.zh: 但经常会发生崩溃，让我犹豫是否要在 Amiga 上做一些我认为理所当然的事情。

blocks.html_en: People ask me all the time when PCs caught up. It didn’t happen all at once. And it wasn’t until 1995 that all the pieces were there.

sentences.en: People ask me all the time when PCs caught up.
sentences.zh: 当个人电脑迎头赶上时，人们总是问我。

sentences.en: It didn’t happen all at once.
sentences.zh: 这并不是一下子发生的。

sentences.en: And it wasn’t until 1995 that all the pieces were there.
sentences.zh: 直到 1995 年，所有的碎片才全部到位。

blocks.html_en: Why the Amiga makes me mad

sentences.en: Why the Amiga makes me mad
sentences.zh: 为什么 Amiga 让我生气

blocks.html_en: And that’s why I almost everything about the Amiga makes me mad. With a good product marketing team behind it, it would have been the greatest computer of all time. When anyone asks its engineers why they built it, they tell you things like they wanted to build the very best computer that they could with the technology that was available to them at the time. That’s how engineers think, so it’s a perfectly good answer, engineer to engineer. But that answer doesn’t sell. You have to come up with something about wanting to change the world. And you have to word it in a way that sounds believable if you are going to attract a mass audience. Apple does that very well. Microsoft was never as good at it, but they were better at it than Commodore.

sentences.en: And that’s why I almost everything about the Amiga makes me mad.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么我几乎对 Amiga 的一切都感到愤怒。

sentences.en: With a good product marketing team behind it, it would have been the greatest computer of all time.
sentences.zh: 如果有优秀的产品营销团队支持，它将成为有史以来最伟大的计算机。

sentences.en: When anyone asks its engineers why they built it, they tell you things like they wanted to build the very best computer that they could with the technology that was available to them at the time.
sentences.zh: 当有人问其工程师为什么要建造它时，他们会告诉你，他们想用当时可用的技术建造最好的计算机。

sentences.en: That’s how engineers think, so it’s a perfectly good answer, engineer to engineer.
sentences.zh: 这就是工程师的想法，所以这是一个完美的答案，工程师对工程师。

sentences.en: But that answer doesn’t sell.
sentences.zh: 但这个答案并不畅销。

sentences.en: You have to come up with something about wanting to change the world.
sentences.zh: 你必须想出一些关于想要改变世界的事情。

sentences.en: And you have to word it in a way that sounds believable if you are going to attract a mass audience.
sentences.zh: 如果你想吸引大量观众，你的措辞必须听起来可信。

sentences.en: Apple does that very well.
sentences.zh: 苹果在这方面做得很好。

sentences.en: Microsoft was never as good at it, but they were better at it than Commodore.
sentences.zh: 微软在这方面从来没有那么擅长，但他们比 Commodore 更擅长。

blocks.html_en: Commodore didn’t have a Steve Jobs or even a Steve Ballmer. They had an oligarch named Irving Gould who was only interested in looting as much from the company as possible as he ran it into the ground. It is a testament to the quality of the product Commodore had that it took him a decade to do it after Jack Tramiel left the company and left him unchecked to rob it blind.

sentences.en: Commodore didn’t have a Steve Jobs or even a Steve Ballmer.
sentences.zh: Commodore 没有史蒂夫·乔布斯，甚至没有史蒂夫·鲍尔默。

sentences.en: They had an oligarch named Irving Gould who was only interested in looting as much from the company as possible as he ran it into the ground.
sentences.zh: 他们有一个名叫欧文·古尔德的寡头，他只对尽可能多地掠夺公司感兴趣，因为他把公司搞砸了。

sentences.en: It is a testament to the quality of the product Commodore had that it took him a decade to do it after Jack Tramiel left the company and left him unchecked to rob it blind.
sentences.zh: 在杰克·特拉米尔（Jack Tramiel）离开公司并让他不受控制地盲目抢劫之后，Commodore花了十年的时间才做到这一点，这证明了Commodore拥有的产品质量。

blocks.html_en: Commodore ran a few ads, but it was clear from their advertising they had no idea what they were selling. The most effective marketers were the people who bought one. Sit down for an hour with an Amiga and someone who knew how to use it, and you’d want one too. But there weren’t enough of us to sustain the platform or the company.

sentences.en: Commodore ran a few ads, but it was clear from their advertising they had no idea what they were selling.
sentences.zh: Commodore 投放了一些广告，但从他们的广告中可以清楚地看出，他们并不知道自己在卖什么。

sentences.en: The most effective marketers were the people who bought one.
sentences.zh: 最有效的营销人员是购买产品的人。

sentences.en: Sit down for an hour with an Amiga and someone who knew how to use it, and you’d want one too.
sentences.zh: 与 Amiga 和知道如何使用它的人一起坐下来一个小时，您也会想要一个。

sentences.en: But there weren’t enough of us to sustain the platform or the company.
sentences.zh: 但我们没有足够的人来维持这个平台或公司。

blocks.html_en: How the Amiga 1000 and its successors were like a kick in the face

sentences.en: How the Amiga 1000 and its successors were like a kick in the face
sentences.zh: Amiga 1000 及其后继产品如何给我们的脸带来一记重击

blocks.html_en: Commodore went bankrupt less than 9 years after introducing the first Amiga. And the company they sold it to went out of business about 15 months after buying it. So instead of getting the greatest computer of all time, it felt like we were getting kicked in the face repeatedly. And then we tried to switch to Apple or Microsoft, and waiting years for them to catch up felt like getting kicked in the face by two people.

sentences.en: Commodore went bankrupt less than 9 years after introducing the first Amiga.
sentences.zh: Commodore 在推出第一款 Amiga 后不到 9 年就破产了。

sentences.en: And the company they sold it to went out of business about 15 months after buying it.
sentences.zh: 他们将其出售给的公司在购买后大约 15 个月就倒闭了。

sentences.en: So instead of getting the greatest computer of all time, it felt like we were getting kicked in the face repeatedly.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们并没有获得有史以来最伟大的计算机，而是感觉自己被不断地踢脸。

sentences.en: And then we tried to switch to Apple or Microsoft, and waiting years for them to catch up felt like getting kicked in the face by two people.
sentences.zh: 然后我们尝试转向苹果或微软，等了几年他们才迎头赶上，感觉就像被两个人踢了脸一样。

blocks.html_en: If you missed out on it, it sounds like I’m being overdramatic. I get it. Nobody knows exactly how many people got to experience it. In the end Commodore sold around 4.91 million Amigas, but at least in the United States, Amiga owners were prone to buy more than one. That makes it hard to say how many distinct owners the machine had. But the people who owned one and lived in the future waiting for the rest of the world to catch up know exactly what I’m talking about.

sentences.en: If you missed out on it, it sounds like I’m being overdramatic.
sentences.zh: 如果你错过了，听起来我就太过戏剧化了。

sentences.en: I get it.
sentences.zh: 我得到它。

sentences.en: Nobody knows exactly how many people got to experience it.
sentences.zh: 没有人确切知道有多少人经历过。

sentences.en: In the end Commodore sold around 4.91 million Amigas, but at least in the United States, Amiga owners were prone to buy more than one.
sentences.zh: 最终 Commodore 售出了大约 491 万辆 Amiga，但至少在美国，Amiga 车主倾向于购买不止一辆。

sentences.en: That makes it hard to say how many distinct owners the machine had.
sentences.zh: 这使得很难说这台机器有多少个不同的所有者。

sentences.en: But the people who owned one and lived in the future waiting for the rest of the world to catch up know exactly what I’m talking about.
sentences.zh: 但那些拥有一台电脑并生活在未来等待世界其他地方赶上的人们确切地知道我在说什么。

blocks.html_en: David Farquhar is a computer security professional, entrepreneur, and author. He has written professionally about computers since 1991, so he was writing about retro computers when they were still new. He has been working in IT professionally since 1994 and has specialized in vulnerability management since 2013. He holds Security+ and CISSP certifications. Today he blogs five times a week, mostly about retro computers and retro gaming covering the time period from 1975 to 2000.

sentences.en: David Farquhar is a computer security professional, entrepreneur, and author.
sentences.zh: David Farquhar 是一位计算机安全专家、企业家和作家。

sentences.en: He has written professionally about computers since 1991, so he was writing about retro computers when they were still new.
sentences.zh: 他自 1991 年起就开始撰写有关计算机的专业文章，因此他在复古计算机还很新的时候就撰写了有关它们的文章。

sentences.en: He has been working in IT professionally since 1994 and has specialized in vulnerability management since 2013.
sentences.zh: 他自 1994 年以来一直从事 IT 专业工作，并自 2013 年以来专门从事漏洞管理。

sentences.en: He holds Security+ and CISSP certifications.
sentences.zh: 他拥有 Security+ 和 CISSP 认证。

sentences.en: Today he blogs five times a week, mostly about retro computers and retro gaming covering the time period from 1975 to 2000.
sentences.zh: 如今，他每周写五次博客，主要是关于 1975 年至 2000 年期间的复古电脑和复古游戏。

content_html_zh:
1985 年 7 月 23 日，Commodore 推出了 Amiga 1000 计算机。 我们只能说世界还没有准备好。 参与后期型号开发的 Commodore 工程师戴夫·海尼 (Dave Haynie) 表示，并没有 20 世纪 80 年代的计算机这样的东西。 有 20 世纪 70 年代的计算机和 1990 年代的计算机，正是 Amiga 将整个行业的其他产品拖入了 1990 年代。

毫不夸张地说，Amiga 1000 领先于时代 10 年。 其定制芯片组具有多达 4,096 种颜色的彩色图形、立体声和图形用户界面。 它的操作系统具有完全抢占式多任务处理能力。 芯片组和操作系统的组合意味着它可以在主频为 7.14 兆赫的 Motorola 68000 CPU 和 256 KB RAM 上运行。 它可以通过单个软盘驱动器启动和运行。 Amiga 于 1984 年 1 月展示了它，当时芯片仅作为原型电路板存在。 到1985年底，它已成为出货产品。

实际上，理想的配置是双软盘驱动器和 512k RAM，如果是 1 MB 就更好了。

但在 1985 年，双软盘和 1 兆 RAM 并不是什么奇怪的机器，而且在这种配置下，它使用起来非常流畅和愉快。 Commodore 委托艺术家安迪·沃霍尔 (Andy Warhol) 进行演示，用它创作数字艺术来展示其功能。

多任务处理：Amiga 1000 的秘密

在 1985 年夏天，图形用户界面并不是什么新鲜事。 苹果公司大约一年半之前就推出了 Macintosh，而 Lisa 甚至早了近一年。 Atari 比 Amiga 早三个月发布了 ST 电脑，ST 还拥有带有鼠标和颜色的图形操作系统。

正是多任务处理能力让 Amiga 名声大噪。 这并不是 Windows 3.0 和 3.1 或 Apple System 7 那样的协作多任务处理。 它是像 Windows 95 一样的完全抢占式多任务处理。 Windows NT 唯一做而 Amiga 没有做的事情就是内存保护。

Amiga 的操作系统不是基于 Unix，当然也不是基于 Windows NT。 它的直接祖先是起源于剑桥大学的名为 TRIPOS 的操作系统。

添加内存保护本来是一个不错的做法，但所需的额外硬件会增加成本。 Amiga 首次亮相时的价格并不便宜。 不带显示器的基本系统售价为 1,295 美元。 一台你肯定想要的彩色显示器，还要花费 300 美元。 添加额外的内存和第二个软盘驱动器，每个驱动器要花费 150 到 200 美元，您很快就会发现您真正想要的 Amiga 更像是一台 2,000 美元的计算机。 生活在未来是昂贵的，但与苹果想要的 2,495 美元购买一台功能较差的机器相比，这个价格并不是不合理。

与 Amiga 一起生活

直到 1987 年，我都不确定自己是否亲眼见过 Amiga，但仅仅通过阅读相关报道，我就知道我想要一台。 直到 1991 年我才实现这一目标，所以我进入这个游戏的时间相当晚了。 但即使在 1991 年，Amiga 仍感觉生活在未来。 我可以加载多个程序并毫不费力地在它们之间切换，唯一的限制是我拥有的内存量。 我可以使用终端程序连接到 BBS，开始下载，然后将其切换到后台，启动文字处理程序，并在下载过程中做作业。 在某些情况下，我什至可以启动游戏并玩游戏，同时在后台进行下载。 我可以一边玩《文明》一边下载东西，这真是太棒了。

最初，Amiga 因不稳定而闻名。 但这主要是因为早期的 Amiga 软件编写得不是很好。 Amiga 软件很容易请求资源，并假设资源可用。 如果请求的资源不可用，它不会优雅地失败，而是简单地分配资源，并引发大师冥想，Amiga 相当于蓝屏死机或内核恐慌。 只是它是红色的，而不是蓝色的。

但不久之后，软件开发人员就学会了如何编写在这个美丽新世界中运行的代码。 当然，当我 1991 年进入现场时，该软件总体上表现得非常好。 当我在 1994 年购买一台 Windows PC 时，感觉就像是一次严重的降级。 如果我把它当作 Amiga 并启动终端程序，连接到 BBS，开始下载，然后切换到 Microsoft Word，它并不总是可靠地工作。 大多数情况下都很好。 但经常会发生崩溃，让我犹豫是否要在 Amiga 上做一些我认为理所当然的事情。

当个人电脑迎头赶上时，人们总是问我。 这并不是一下子发生的。 直到 1995 年，所有的碎片才全部到位。

为什么 Amiga 让我生气

这就是为什么我几乎对 Amiga 的一切都感到愤怒。 如果有优秀的产品营销团队支持，它将成为有史以来最伟大的计算机。 当有人问其工程师为什么要建造它时，他们会告诉你，他们想用当时可用的技术建造最好的计算机。 这就是工程师的想法，所以这是一个完美的答案，工程师对工程师。 但这个答案并不畅销。 你必须想出一些关于想要改变世界的事情。 如果你想吸引大量观众，你的措辞必须听起来可信。 苹果在这方面做得很好。 微软在这方面从来没有那么擅长，但他们比 Commodore 更擅长。

Commodore 没有史蒂夫·乔布斯，甚至没有史蒂夫·鲍尔默。 他们有一个名叫欧文·古尔德的寡头，他只对尽可能多地掠夺公司感兴趣，因为他把公司搞砸了。 在杰克·特拉米尔（Jack Tramiel）离开公司并让他不受控制地盲目抢劫之后，Commodore花了十年的时间才做到这一点，这证明了Commodore拥有的产品质量。

Commodore 投放了一些广告，但从他们的广告中可以清楚地看出，他们并不知道自己在卖什么。 最有效的营销人员是购买产品的人。 与 Amiga 和知道如何使用它的人一起坐下来一个小时，您也会想要一个。 但我们没有足够的人来维持这个平台或公司。

Amiga 1000 及其后继产品如何给我们的脸带来一记重击

Commodore 在推出第一款 Amiga 后不到 9 年就破产了。 他们将其出售给的公司在购买后大约 15 个月就倒闭了。 因此，我们并没有获得有史以来最伟大的计算机，而是感觉自己被不断地踢脸。 然后我们尝试转向苹果或微软，等了几年他们才迎头赶上，感觉就像被两个人踢了脸一样。

如果你错过了，听起来我就太过戏剧化了。 我得到它。 没有人确切知道有多少人经历过。 最终 Commodore 售出了大约 491 万辆 Amiga，但至少在美国，Amiga 车主倾向于购买不止一辆。 这使得很难说这台机器有多少个不同的所有者。 但那些拥有一台电脑并生活在未来等待世界其他地方赶上的人们确切地知道我在说什么。

David Farquhar 是一位计算机安全专家、企业家和作家。 他自 1991 年起就开始撰写有关计算机的专业文章，因此他在复古计算机还很新的时候就撰写了有关它们的文章。 他自 1994 年以来一直从事 IT 专业工作，并自 2013 年以来专门从事漏洞管理。 他拥有 Security+ 和 CISSP 认证。 如今，他每周写五次博客，主要是关于 1975 年至 2000 年期间的复古电脑和复古游戏。
